content.text: How do I securely implement a function to allow existing users to change their passwords?
What happens if the initial user object (`this.user`) cannot be found or accessed at the start of the function?
The code uses a try-except block targeting `self.user_model.DoesNotExist`; if this occurs, it calls `self.auth_failed()` immediately.
That makes sense, thanks for clarifying how it handles failures!
